backlog.title: Host-Side Rules & Typed Commands
backlog.description: Implement the typed-command namespace (§4.6), host-authoritative SET_OS (§4.7), and the host-side-rules machinery (§14) in notifier.{c,h}, with host test coverage and README docs — while leaving the legacy string path, the pattern matcher, multi-OS selection, and all prior tests byte-for-byte unchanged. This delta promotes the previously planned v0.3.0 host-rules feature to implemented. Baseline: 2023/2023 pattern_match tests + 31/31 notifier stub tests green.

milestones.title: Public API Surface + State Scaffolding
milestones.description: The header additions (host_callback_t, DEFINE_HOST_CALLBACKS, typed-command constants) and the notifier.c globals, weak accessors, board_rules_present helper, and the apply_os_change seam refactor. After this milestone the module compiles with the new surface but typed commands are not yet dispatched.

tasks.title: notifier.h host-rules API surface
tasks.description: Add host_callback_t struct, host-callback accessors, DEFINE_HOST_CALLBACKS macro, and typed-command constants to the public header.

subtasks.title: Add host_callback_t, DEFINE_HOST_CALLBACKS macro, host-callback accessors, and typed-command constants to notifier.h
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_16d737de7a3e/architecture/host_rules_architecture.md § 'The four typed-command handlers' and plan/003_16d737de7a3e/architecture/external_deps.md § 'Command table'. The existing notifier.h (91 lines) has command_map_t/layer_map_t, DEFINE_SERIAL_*(_OS) macros, notifier_set_os, WT, constants. callback_t is already typedef'd as `typedef void (*callback_t)(void);`.
2. INPUT: No code dependencies — this is the foundational header surface. The existing notifier.h struct definitions and macro patterns (DEFINE_SERIAL_COMMANDS) serve as the template for the new DEFINE_HOST_CALLBACKS macro.
3. LOGIC: Add to notifier.h AFTER the existing layer_map_t typedef and BEFORE the DEFINE_SERIAL macros: (a) `typedef struct { const char *name; callback_t on_enable; callback_t on_disable; } host_callback_t;` per §14. (b) Accessor declarations: `host_callback_t* get_host_callbacks(void); size_t get_host_callbacks_size(void);` per §14. (c) `#define DEFINE_HOST_CALLBACKS(...)` macro that defines `user_host_callbacks[]` + the matching accessor pair `get_host_callbacks()`/`get_host_callbacks_size()`, overriding weak defaults in notifier.c — mirror the exact DEFINE_SERIAL_COMMANDS pattern (the array + sizeof/sizeof + accessor pair). (d) Typed-command constants block: `#define NOTIFY_CMD_DISCRIMINATOR 0xF0`, `#define NOTIFY_RESPONSE_MARKER 0x51`, `#define NOTIFY_CMD_QUERY_INFO 0x01`, `#define NOTIFY_CMD_QUERY_CALLBACK 0x02`, `#define NOTIFY_CMD_SET_OS 0x03`, `#define NOTIFY_CMD_APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT 0x05`, `#define NOTIFY_PROTO_VER 2`, feature-flag bit defines (`#define NOTIFY_FEATURE_APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT 0x01`, `#define NOTIFY_FEATURE_CALLBACK_REGISTRY 0x02`, `#define NOTIFY_FEATURE_VIA_COEXIST 0x04`), `#define HOST_CALLBACK_MAX 32`, `#define HOST_LAYER_BASE 224` per §4.6/§16.
4. OUTPUT: A keymap writing DEFINE_HOST_CALLBACKS({...}) at file scope generates user_host_callbacks[] + strong accessors that override notifier.c's weak defaults at link time. A keymap that omits it links and behaves identically to today (structural backward-compat). The typed-command constants are available for notifier.c to #include.
5. DOCS (Mode A): Inline comments on each constant block citing its PRD section (§4.6 for discriminator/marker/cmd_ids/proto_ver/feature_flags, §14 for HOST_CALLBACK_MAX and host layer base, §16 for values). Comment on DEFINE_HOST_CALLBACKS noting 'ID = array index, stable per build; host re-queries names on reconnect so cross-flash renumbering is harmless (§14)'. This rides WITH the work, do not create a separate docs subtask.

tasks.title: notifier.c state scaffolding + weak accessors + apply_os_change seam
tasks.description: Add host_layer/host_cb_enabled[]/has_been_queried globals, weak host-callback accessors, board_rules_present helper, and refactor notifier_set_os into apply_os_change shared seam.

subtasks.title: Add host state globals, weak host-callback accessors, and board_rules_present helper to notifier.c
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_16d737de7a3e/architecture/host_rules_architecture.md § 'two independent state planes' and findings_and_risks.md F8 (board_rules_present must check ALL maps). The existing notifier.c globals region (notifier.c:~105-120) has activated_layer, current_command, current_os. The weak-default pattern for board maps (notifier.c:~130-165) is the template for the new host-callback weak accessors.
2. INPUT: The host_callback_t type + HOST_CALLBACK_MAX + LAYER_UNSET (255) from P1.M1.T1.S1 (notifier.h). The existing per-OS accessor functions (_notifier_get_*_map_OS_*) for the board_rules_present aggregation.
3. LOGIC: Add to notifier.c globals region near activated_layer/current_os (§8.1 region): (a) `static uint8_t host_layer = LAYER_UNSET;` — the host layer tracker, independent of board activated_layer (§14). (b) `static bool host_cb_enabled[HOST_CALLBACK_MAX];` — zero-initialized; the single source of truth for which host callbacks are currently enabled (the diff target of apply_host_callbacks in P1.M2). (c) `static bool has_been_queried = false;` — set on first QUERY_INFO service (§4.6 handshake timing). (d) Weak-default host-callback accessors mirroring the existing board-map weak block: `__attribute__((weak)) host_callback_t* get_host_callbacks(void) { return NULL; }` and `__attribute__((weak)) size_t get_host_callbacks_size(void) { return 0; }`. (e) `static bool board_rules_present(void)` helper returning true iff ANY board map is non-empty: check get_command_map_size()>0 || get_layer_map_size()>0 || for each OS (LINUX/WINDOWS/MACOS/IOS) check _notifier_get_command_map_OS_*_size()>0 || _notifier_get_layer_map_OS_*_size()>0. Do NOT expose per-OS granularity (§4.6 'a single bit suffices').
4. OUTPUT: The globals/accessors exist and compile. host_cb_enabled[] is the diff target for apply_host_callbacks. board_rules_present() is callable by QUERY_INFO. Weak defaults keep a no-callback keymap linking + behaving identically. host_layer is ready for set_host_layer in P1.M2.
5. DOCS (Mode A): Comment each global with its PRD anchor: host_layer 'independent of board activated_layer (§14)', has_been_queried '§4.6 handshake-timing rule', host_cb_enabled[] 'diff target for apply_host_callbacks (§14)'. Comment the weak accessor block: 'overridden by DEFINE_HOST_CALLBACKS; {NULL,0} ⇒ no registry ⇒ feature_flags & 0x02 clear'. This rides WITH the work.

subtasks.title: Refactor notifier_set_os into shared apply_os_change seam for SET_OS reuse
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_16d737de7a3e/architecture/host_rules_architecture.md § 'The apply_os_change seam refactor' and findings_and_risks.md F2 (SET_OS must share the notifier_set_os seam). The current notifier_set_os (notifier.c:~470-485) contains F9 clear-on-change logic (idempotent check → disable_command → deactivate_layer).
2. INPUT: The existing notifier_set_os function body in notifier.c. The os_variant_t type from os_detection.h.
3. LOGIC: Extract the body of notifier_set_os into a new `static void apply_os_change(os_variant_t os)` function: `if (os == current_os) return;` (idempotent F9.3) then `current_os = os; disable_command(); deactivate_layer();` (F9.1 clear). Replace notifier_set_os with a one-line forwarder: `void notifier_set_os(os_variant_t os) { apply_os_change(os); }`. The CONSOLE_ENABLE uprintf stays in apply_os_change. This function becomes the SOLE mutation point for current_os (besides init), called by both notifier_set_os (keymap) and SET_OS (typed cmd, implemented in P1.M2.T3.S2). Do NOT duplicate the F9 logic in the SET_OS handler.
4. OUTPUT: apply_os_change() is callable from both the public notifier_set_os (keymap path) and the SET_OS handler (P1.M2.T3.S2). The existing test_notifier_os tests pass unchanged (notifier_set_os behavior is identical — it just delegates now).
5. DOCS (Mode A): Comment apply_os_change: 'Shared seam for OS changes from both the keymap (notifier_set_os) and the host (SET_OS typed cmd). Sole mutation point for current_os (invariant 17). Idempotent on unchanged value (F9.3); clears state on change (F9.1). Does NOT re-dispatch the last message (F9.2).' This rides WITH the work.

milestones.title: Typed Dispatch + Host State Machines
milestones.description: The core implementation: route 0xF0 commands in hid_notify, the host state machines (set_host_layer, apply_host_callbacks), and the four typed-command handlers + response builder.

tasks.title: hid_notify typed routing + multi-report framing + host state machines
tasks.description: Add the typed_mode flag and discriminator check to hid_notify for routing typed commands vs legacy strings, plus the set_host_layer and apply_host_callbacks host state machines.

subtasks.title: Add typed_mode flag + 0xF0 discriminator routing fork in hid_notify
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_16d737de7a3e/architecture/host_rules_architecture.md § 'Dispatch fork in hid_notify' and findings_and_risks.md F7 (multi-report typed framing uses existing reassembly) + RISK-1 (typed_mode lifecycle). The current hid_notify (notifier.c:~490-540) has: coexistence guard → strip magic → byte loop into msg_buffer until ETX → process_full_message → send 0/1 ack.
2. INPUT: The NOTIFY_CMD_DISCRIMINATOR (0xF0) constant from P1.M1.T1.S1. The existing msg_buffer/msg_index/dropping reassembly machinery. The handle_typed_command() function to be implemented in P1.M2.T3 — for now stub it as a forward declaration returning a uint8_t (the typed response byte for response[0], or a bool) so this subtask compiles.
3. LOGIC: (a) Add `static bool typed_mode = false;` near msg_index (persists across hid_notify calls for multi-report messages). (b) In hid_notify, after the coexistence guard (data[0]!=0x81 check) and BEFORE stripping the header, add: `if (msg_index == 0 && length >= 3 && data[2] == NOTIFY_CMD_DISCRIMINATOR) { typed_mode = true; }`. The check at msg_index==0 ensures the discriminator is only read from the FIRST report (subsequent report bytes at data[2] may coincidentally be 0xF0). (c) Strip the magic header and accumulate into msg_buffer exactly as today — the discriminator byte (0xF0) and cmd_id byte naturally become msg_buffer[0] and msg_buffer[1]. (d) On ETX: if typed_mode is true, call `bool typed_result = handle_typed_command(msg_buffer);` and set `match = typed_result;` (the typed path sends its own [0x51] response inside handle_typed_command — so hid_notify does NOT send the legacy 0/1 ack for typed commands; instead the response-sending is owned by handle_typed_command). For the legacy path, the behavior is UNCHANGED: `match = process_full_message(msg_buffer); response[0] = match ? 1 : 0; raw_hid_send(response, RAW_REPORT_SIZE);`. (e) Reset typed_mode = false at EVERY ETX boundary (normal dispatch, dropping, typed dispatch) alongside msg_index = 0 — this is RISK-1 mitigation. Also reset typed_mode=false when dropping triggers (overflow path).
4. OUTPUT: 0xF0 messages bypass process_full_message (no board disable/deactivate side effects). Legacy string messages (data[2] printable, never 0xF0) take the unchanged path — test_notifier_dispatch and test_notifier_os pass UNCHANGED. Multi-report typed commands reassemble via existing msg_buffer + typed_mode flag.
5. DOCS (Mode A): Comment the routing branch citing §4.6 (discriminator, ETX framing, bypass of board side effects) and the typed_mode/msg_index==0 first-report rule. Note RISK-1 mitigation (reset typed_mode at all ETX boundaries). This rides WITH the work.

subtasks.title: Implement set_host_layer (host layer tracker, independent of board activated_layer)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_16d737de7a3e/architecture/host_rules_architecture.md § 'two independent state planes' and findings_and_risks.md RISK-2 (host/board layer collision — none because different variables). The existing activate_layer/deactivate_layer (notifier.c:~175-195) wrap layer_on/layer_off for the board tracker; set_host_layer does the same for the host tracker.
2. INPUT: The host_layer global from P1.M1.T2.S1 (initialized to LAYER_UNSET=255). The LAYER_UNSET constant. QMK's layer_on/layer_off functions (stub-provided in tests).
3. LOGIC: Implement `static void set_host_layer(uint8_t layer)` per §14: (a) If layer == 0xFF (LAYER_UNSET): if host_layer != LAYER_UNSET, call layer_off(host_layer); set host_layer = LAYER_UNSET. (b) Otherwise (a real host layer, expected ≥ 224): if host_layer != LAYER_UNSET, call layer_off(host_layer) first (turn off the old host layer); then call layer_on(layer); set host_layer = layer. (c) This function touches ONLY the host_layer tracker — it NEVER modifies the board activated_layer variable. It does NOT check or clear board state (that's clear_board's job in APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT).
4. OUTPUT: set_host_layer() is callable by the APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT handler (P1.M2.T3.S3). Host layers (≥224) stack above board layers under QMK's highest-layer-wins rule. The stub harness tracks the active layer via g_active_layer for test assertions.
5. DOCS (Mode A): Comment set_host_layer: 'Host layer tracker ONLY — operates on host_layer, independent of board activated_layer (§14). 0xFF clears the host layer. Host layers reserved ≥ 224 (HOST_LAYER_BASE).' This rides WITH the work.

subtasks.title: Implement apply_host_callbacks (disable-before-enable diff against host_cb_enabled[])
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_16d737de7a3e/architecture/host_rules_architecture.md § 'Host callback diff algorithm' and findings_and_risks.md RISK-3 (id range validation). The existing disable_command/enable_command (notifier.c:~200-220) show the NULL-callback guard pattern.
2. INPUT: The host_cb_enabled[] array (HOST_CALLBACK_MAX bools, zero-initialized) from P1.M1.T2.S1. The get_host_callbacks()/get_host_callbacks_size() accessors from P1.M1.T2.S1.
3. LOGIC: Implement `static void apply_host_callbacks(const uint8_t *ids, uint8_t count)` per §14. PHASE 1 — DISABLE (newly-out-of-set, fires on_disable FIRST): for id in 0..HOST_CALLBACK_MAX-1: if host_cb_enabled[id] is true AND id is NOT in ids[0..count): resolve the callback via get_host_callbacks()[id] (only if id < get_host_callbacks_size() — defensive guard, RISK-3); if its on_disable is non-NULL, call it; set host_cb_enabled[id] = false. PHASE 2 — ENABLE (newly-in-set, fires on_enable AFTER all disables): for i in 0..count-1: id = ids[i]; if id < HOST_CALLBACK_MAX AND id < get_host_callbacks_size() AND NOT host_cb_enabled[id]: resolve get_host_callbacks()[id]; if its on_enable is non-NULL, call it; set host_cb_enabled[id] = true. The disable-before-enable ordering mirrors the board's disable_command/enable_command sequence (invariant 4). NULL on_enable/on_disable guarded (same pattern as enable_command/disable_command).
4. OUTPUT: apply_host_callbacks() is callable by the APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT handler (P1.M2.T3.S3). The host_cb_enabled[] array is the single source of truth for which host callbacks are enabled. Disable-before-enable prevents a callback being briefly in both states during a transition.
5. DOCS (Mode A): Comment apply_host_callbacks: 'Mirrors board disable-before-enable ordering (§14, invariant 4). Phase 1 disables newly-out ids (fires on_disable), Phase 2 enables newly-in ids (fires on_enable). Defensive id range check (RISK-3).' This rides WITH the work.

tasks.title: handle_typed_command + four handlers + typed response builder
tasks.description: Implement the typed-command dispatch function and the QUERY_INFO, QUERY_CALLBACK, SET_OS, and APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT handlers, plus the [0x51] typed response builder.

subtasks.title: Implement typed response builder + handle_typed_command dispatch + QUERY_INFO + QUERY_CALLBACK handlers
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_16d737de7a3e/architecture/host_rules_architecture.md § 'The four typed-command handlers' and external_deps.md § 'Command table'. The reassembled msg_buffer layout for a typed command: msg_buffer[0]==0xF0 (discriminator), [1]==cmd_id, [2..]==args, NUL-terminated after ETX strip.
2. INPUT: The typed-command constants from P1.M1.T1.S1 (NOTIFY_RESPONSE_MARKER 0x51, NOTIFY_CMD_QUERY_INFO 0x01, NOTIFY_CMD_QUERY_CALLBACK 0x02, NOTIFY_PROTO_VER 2, feature flags). The board_rules_present() helper and has_been_queried bool from P1.M1.T2.S1. The get_host_callbacks()/get_host_callbacks_size() accessors. RAW_REPORT_SIZE (32). The msg_buffer content (the reassembled typed command).
3. LOGIC: (a) RESPONSE BUILDER: `static void send_typed_response(uint8_t cmd_id, const uint8_t *payload, uint8_t payload_len)` — fills a 32-byte response: response[0]=NOTIFY_RESPONSE_MARKER (0x51), response[1]=cmd_id, memcpy payload into response[2..2+payload_len], zero-pad the rest, then raw_hid_send(response, RAW_REPORT_SIZE). (b) HANDLE_TYPED_COMMAND: `static bool handle_typed_command(char *data)` — data[0] is 0xF0, data[1] is cmd_id, data[2..] is args. Switch on (uint8_t)data[1]. (c) QUERY_INFO (0x01): set has_been_queried = true (§4.6 handshake). Build payload: proto_ver=NOTIFY_PROTO_VER, feature_flags = NOTIFY_FEATURE_APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT | (get_host_callbacks_size()>0 ? NOTIFY_FEATURE_CALLBACK_REGISTRY : 0), callback_count = (uint8_t)get_host_callbacks_size(), board_rules_present = board_rules_present() ? 1 : 0. Send via send_typed_response(0x01, payload, 4). Return true (typed path always succeeds — it sent a response). (d) QUERY_CALLBACK (0x02): args[0] = index. If index < get_host_callbacks_size(): build payload [index, name bytes from get_host_callbacks()[index].name (NUL-padded to fill up to 30 bytes)], send_typed_response(0x02, payload, len). Else: payload = [index, 0x00] (name absent, §4.6), send_typed_response(0x02, payload, 2). Return true. (e) Default/unknown cmd_id (incl 0x04 reserved): send_typed_response with just [0x51][cmd_id] (no payload), return true. Never crash.
4. OUTPUT: QUERY_INFO advertises proto_ver=2 + feature_flags + callback_count + board_rules_present. QUERY_CALLBACK returns callback names by index. has_been_queried flips on first QUERY_INFO. Typed responses carry the 0x51 marker. Unknown commands handled gracefully.
5. DOCS (Mode A): Comment handle_typed_command and each handler with its §4.6 anchor, the response byte layout, and the handshake semantics. Comment send_typed_response: 'Always sends 32 bytes with 0x51 marker (distinct from legacy 0/1 ack, §4.6).' This rides WITH the work.

subtasks.title: Implement SET_OS handler (host-authoritative OS via apply_os_change seam)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_16d737de7a3e/architecture/host_rules_architecture.md § 'SET_OS (0x03) — host-authoritative OS' and findings_and_risks.md F2 (SET_OS shares notifier_set_os seam). The apply_os_change() function from P1.M1.T2.S2 contains the F9 clear-on-change logic. os_variant_t values: OS_UNSURE=0, OS_LINUX=1, OS_WINDOWS=2, OS_MACOS=3, OS_IOS=4.
2. INPUT: The apply_os_change(os_variant_t os) function from P1.M1.T2.S2. The NOTIFY_CMD_SET_OS (0x03) constant. The msg_buffer args: data[2] = os_byte (0=UNSURE, 1=LINUX, 2=WINDOWS, 3=MACOS, 4=IOS per §4.6).
3. LOGIC: Add SET_OS case (0x03) to handle_typed_command's switch: args[0] = (uint8_t)data[2] (the os_byte). Cast to os_variant_t and call `apply_os_change((os_variant_t)os_byte)`. This routes through the SAME function as notifier_set_os, so an OS change triggers F9 clear (idempotent check → disable_command → deactivate_layer). Do NOT duplicate F9 logic. Build response payload [ack=1] and send_typed_response(0x03, payload, 1). Return true.
4. OUTPUT: SET_OS updates current_os authoritatively (host value wins over OS_DETECTION heuristic while host is connected, §4.7). An OS change clears state per F9 (idempotent on unchanged value — no spurious clear). Response is [0x51][0x03][0x01].
5. DOCS (Mode A): Comment the SET_OS handler: 'Host-authoritative OS while connected (§4.7). Routes through apply_os_change — same seam as notifier_set_os, so F9 clear-on-change is not duplicated. os_byte mirrors os_variant_t (0=UNSURE..4=IOS).' This rides WITH the work.

subtasks.title: Implement APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT handler (clear_board + set_host_layer + apply_host_callbacks)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_16d737de7a3e/architecture/host_rules_architecture.md § 'APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT (0x05)' and external_deps.md § 'Field definitions' for the args layout. The set_host_layer() from P1.M2.T1.S2 and apply_host_callbacks() from P1.M2.T1.S3 are the state machines this handler drives.
2. INPUT: The NOTIFY_CMD_APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT (0x05) constant. set_host_layer(uint8_t) from P1.M2.T1.S2. apply_host_callbacks(const uint8_t*, uint8_t) from P1.M2.T1.S3. The deactivate_layer()/disable_command() board functions. The msg_buffer args layout: data[2]=layer (host layer, 0xFF=LAYER_UNSET to clear), data[3]=flags (bit 0 = clear_board), data[4]=count, data[5..5+count-1]=callback ids.
3. LOGIC: Add APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT case (0x05) to handle_typed_command's switch. Parse args: layer = (uint8_t)data[2], flags = (uint8_t)data[3], count = (uint8_t)data[4], ids = (uint8_t*)&data[5] (pointer into the reassembled buffer). If count > 0 and data length is sufficient: (a) If flags & 0x01 (clear_board): call deactivate_layer() (board) + disable_command() (board) BEFORE applying — this is the per-window 'replace' decision (§14). Else ('stack'): leave board state untouched. (b) Call set_host_layer(layer) — 0xFF clears the host layer. (c) Call apply_host_callbacks(ids, count) — disable-before-enable diff. Build response payload [ack=1] and send_typed_response(0x05, payload, 1). Return true. Guard against insufficient args length (count exceeds available bytes → clamp or skip callbacks defensively).
4. OUTPUT: APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT honors clear_board (replace: clears board layer+command first; stack: leaves board untouched) then applies host layer + callbacks. Response is [0x51][0x05][0x01]. The board and host state planes remain independent except for the explicit clear_board action.
5. DOCS (Mode A): Comment the APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT handler: 'Per-window stack/replace via clear_board flag (§14). clear_board=1: deactivate board layer + disable board command BEFORE applying (replace). clear_board=0: board state untouched (stack). Then set_host_layer + apply_host_callbacks.' This rides WITH the work.

milestones.title: Host Tests + Acceptance Gate + Changeset Docs
milestones.description: Dedicated host test coverage for the typed-command namespace + host rules, the extended stub harness, the runner, the full §11.2 acceptance gate, and the README sync.

tasks.title: Stub response capture + test_notifier_host.c
tasks.description: Extend the stub harness to capture the full 32-byte response for typed-response assertions, then write the dedicated host test suite covering all typed-command and host-rules categories.

subtasks.title: Extend qmk_stubs.c to capture full 32-byte response; add stub_get_last_response() accessor
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_16d737de7a3e/architecture/findings_and_risks.md F6 (stub observables precedent). The current qmk_stubs/qmk_stubs.c (26 lines) has raw_hid_send that only prints response[0]. The stub_get_active_layer() observable was added in plan 002 for multi-OS test assertions.
2. INPUT: The current qmk_stubs.c with its raw_hid_send implementation.
3. LOGIC: Add to qmk_stubs.c: (a) `static uint8_t g_last_response[32];` file-static. (b) In raw_hid_send, add `memcpy(g_last_response, data, (length < 32) ? length : 32);` before the existing debug print. (c) Expose `const uint8_t* stub_get_last_response(void) { return g_last_response; }` as a test-harness observable. This is a TEST-HARNESS enhancement, NOT production code (mirrors stub_get_active_layer precedent).
4. OUTPUT: Host tests can assert the typed-response marker 0x51 (stub_get_last_response()[0]), the cmd_echo ([1]), and payload bytes ([2], [3], etc.) for QUERY_INFO/QUERY_CALLBACK/SET_OS/APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT responses.
5. DOCS (Mode A): Comment the capture as 'Test-harness observable (not production); mirrors stub_get_active_layer precedent (F6).' This rides WITH the work.

subtasks.title: Write test_notifier_host.c: QUERY_INFO + QUERY_CALLBACK test cases
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_16d737de7a3e/architecture/host_rules_architecture.md § 'Test strategy'. The existing test_notifier_dispatch.c pattern: file-scope DEFINE_* macros, ck() helper printing PASS:/FAIL:, g_pass/g_fail counters, summary line, return g_fail?1:0. The runner greps `grep -c '^FAIL:'`.
2. INPUT: The full notifier.c implementation from P1.M2 (routing, framing, handlers, state machines). DEFINE_HOST_CALLBACKS from P1.M1.T1.S1. The stub response capture stub_get_last_response() from P1.M3.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Create test_notifier_host.c that stub-links notifier.c (same -DQMK_KEYBOARD_H='"qmk_keyboard_stub.h"' -Iqmk_stubs -I. flags). Define DEFINE_HOST_CALLBACKS({...}) and DEFINE_SERIAL_COMMANDS({...})/DEFINE_SERIAL_LAYERS({...}) at file scope (distinguishable callbacks that set global flags, following F6 precedent). Implement test helper to send a typed command report via hid_notify and assert response bytes. QUERY_INFO tests: (i) send [0x81][0x9F][0xF0][0x01][0x03 ETX] → assert response[0]==0x51, response[1]==0x01, response[2]==2 (proto_ver), response[3] has bit 0 and bit 1 set (features), response[4]==callback_count from DEFINE_HOST_CALLBACKS, response[5]==1 (board_rules_present true since DEFINE_SERIAL_* defined). (ii) has_been_queried flips (verify indirectly — e.g. by checking it doesn't clear board state on a second QUERY_INFO). QUERY_CALLBACK tests: (iii) send QUERY_CALLBACK(0) → assert response[0]==0x51, response[1]==0x02, response[2]==0 (index), response[3..]==first callback name bytes. (iv) QUERY_CALLBACK(out_of_range) → assert response[2]==index, response[3]==0x00 (name absent).
4. OUTPUT: test_notifier_host.c compiles with `gcc -DQMK_KEYBOARD_H='"qmk_keyboard_stub.h"' -Iqmk_stubs -I. notifier.c qmk_stubs/qmk_stubs.c test_notifier_host.c -std=c99` and the QUERY_INFO + QUERY_CALLBACK tests pass with 0 FAIL: lines.
5. DOCS (Mode A): Header comment mapping each test block to its §4.6 criterion. This rides WITH the work.

subtasks.title: Add SET_OS + APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT (stack/replace) test cases to test_notifier_host.c
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_16d737de7a3e/architecture/host_rules_architecture.md § 'SET_OS' and § 'APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT'. The test_notifier_os.c F9 clear-on-change test pattern is the template for SET_OS change-detection tests. The existing DEFINE_*_OS pattern enables verifying that SET_OS changes which OS map is selected.
2. INPUT: The test_notifier_host.c file from P1.M3.T1.S2 (with its DEFINE_* macros and helpers). The notifier.c SET_OS and APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT handlers from P1.M2.T2.S2 and P1.M2.T2.S3. stub_get_active_layer() for layer assertions.
3. LOGIC: Add to test_notifier_host.c: SET_OS tests: (i) send SET_OS(OS_MACOS) → assert response[0]==0x51, response[1]==0x03, response[2]==1 (ack). (ii) Verify current_os changed: send a legacy string that matches only the OS_MACOS map → assert the OS_MACOS rule fires (not the default). (iii) SET_OS change fires F9 clear: set up a board layer via legacy string, then SET_OS to a different OS → assert the board layer was deactivated (stub_get_active_layer reflects the clear) and the previous command's on_disable fired. (iv) SET_OS idempotent: SET_OS to the same OS → no spurious on_disable/deactivate. APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT tests: (v) Stack (clear_board=0): set board layer via legacy string, then APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT{layer=224, flags=0, count=0} → assert board layer still active AND host layer 224 active (via stub_get_active_layer). (vi) Replace (clear_board=1): set board layer via legacy string, then APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT{layer=224, flags=0x01, count=0} → assert board layer deactivated, host layer 224 active. (vii) Callback diff: APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT with callback ids [0] → assert on_enable fires for id 0; then APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT with ids [1] → assert on_disable fires for id 0 BEFORE on_enable fires for id 1 (disable-before-enable ordering). (viii) APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT{layer=0xFF} clears host layer.
4. OUTPUT: SET_OS and APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT tests pass with 0 FAIL: lines. All §4.6/§4.7/§14 criteria for these two commands are exercised.
5. DOCS (Mode A): Comment each test block with its §4.6/§4.7/§14 criterion. This rides WITH the work.

subtasks.title: Add coexistence/backward-compat + multi-report typed framing test cases to test_notifier_host.c
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_16d737de7a3e/architecture/findings_and_risks.md F5 (existing tests never send 0xF0) and F7 (multi-report typed framing). The invariant: legacy strings have data[2] printable (0x20-0x7E), never 0xF0, so the typed routing branch never fires for them.
2. INPUT: The test_notifier_host.c file from P1.M3.T1.S3.
3. LOGIC: Add to test_notifier_host.c: (i) COEXISTENCE/BACKWARD-COMPAT: send a legacy string report (e.g. [0x81][0x9F]['f','i','r','e','f','o','x',0x03]) → assert it routes to process_full_message (response[0] is 0 or 1, NOT 0x51) — i.e. test_notifier_dispatch-style behavior is intact alongside the typed path. (ii) Send a non-magic report (data[0]!=0x81) → assert it is silently discarded (no response side effects). (iii) MULTI-REPORT TYPED FRAMING: send a typed command split across two 32-byte reports — the first with [0x81][0x9F][0xF0][cmd_id][partial args...] and the second with [remaining args...][0x03 ETX] → assert it reassembles and dispatches correctly (exercised with a large APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT id list or a QUERY_CALLBACK that fills the buffer). The discriminator 0xF0 appears only in the first report.
4. OUTPUT: The full test_notifier_host.c covers all six categories (QUERY_INFO, QUERY_CALLBACK, SET_OS, APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT stack/replace, coexistence/backward-compat, multi-report framing). All pass with 0 FAIL: lines. The test suite summary prints totals in the standard format.
5. DOCS (Mode A): Comment the coexistence tests citing §4.6 (0xF0 never begins a real matched string; sanitizer allows only 0x20-0x7E) and the multi-report tests citing §4.6 (ETX-framed, may span reports). This rides WITH the work.

subtasks.title: Add Host-Side Rules & Typed Commands section to README.md; update Setup / Running Tests / What-this-does-NOT-change
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_16d737de7a3e/delta_prd.md § 'P1.M3.T3'. The current README.md has a Multi-OS Configuration section (plan 002); the host-rules feature is entirely absent. This is a Mode B (changeset-level) documentation task — it depends on every implementing subtask.
2. INPUT: All implementing subtasks complete and verified green (dependencies list all P1.M1, P1.M2, and P1.M3.T1/T2 subtasks). The full feature as implemented: notifier.h API (host_callback_t, DEFINE_HOST_CALLBACKS, typed-command constants), notifier.c (handle_typed_command, four handlers, host state machines), test_notifier_host.c (host tests), run_notifier_stub_tests.sh (extended runner).
3. LOGIC (Mode B — cross-cutting summary): (a) Add a 'Host-Side Rules & Typed Commands' section documenting: the opt-in nature (a keymap without DEFINE_HOST_CALLBACKS is unchanged); DEFINE_HOST_CALLBACKS + the host_callback_t row shape ({name, on_enable, on_disable}); the typed-command namespace at a glance (QUERY_INFO/QUERY_CALLBACK/SET_OS/APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT, the 0xF0 discriminator, 0x51 responses, capability handshake + proto_ver=2); the per-window stack vs replace model via clear_board (driven by the host's per-rule disable_firmware_config, pointing to qmkonnect/spec/HOST_RULES.md for the host-side design); host-authoritative SET_OS (host value wins over OS_DETECTION while connected); host layers reserved ≥ 224. (b) Setup note: host-rules users add DEFINE_HOST_CALLBACKS({...}) and the host (QMKonnect) negotiates via QUERY_INFO. (c) Running Tests: mention test_notifier_host is now built+run by run_notifier_stub_tests.sh. (d) 'What this does NOT change': the legacy string wire protocol and the pattern matcher are untouched; board DEFINE_*/DEFINE_*_OS rules keep working (stacked or replaced per the host's per-window decision).
4. OUTPUT: README documents the host-rules feature end-to-end + the backward-compat guarantee. A user understands how a host ruleset coexists with board rules.
5. DOCS: This IS the changeset-level documentation task (Mode B, depends on every implementing subtask).
